Title: Computationally Guided Design of a Soluble, Abundant and Functional AnfH Variant for the Expression of Fe-Only Nitrogenase in Plant Mitochondria

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioavailable reduced nitrogen for plant nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports FeS cluster reconstitution/loading of plant-produced AnfH, restoring activity of NbAnfH V6 in nitrogenase assays.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A. vinelandii NifU was co-expressed with AnfH in plants as a candidate maturation factor. 2. EcNifU purified from E. coli and chemically reconstituted with iron and sulfide successfully activated NbAnfH V6 in vitro. 3. In planta co-expression was insufficient to yield active holo-AnfH, indicating FeS loading remains limiting.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with AnfH in N. benthamiana; heterologously expressed in E. coli for purification
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression; purification from E. coli; chemical FeS reconstitution; in vitro activation assay of AnfH
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV enhanced 35S promoter in plants, constitutive; T7 promoter in E. coli
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory factor for reconstructing nitrogenase maturation in plants, but alone insufficient for effective in planta FeS loading of AnfH.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Did not solve in planta holo-AnfH formation; may require additional maturation factors or different assembly context.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as maturation scaffold/donor rather than focal catalytic nitrogenase protein in this paper.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioavailable reduced nitrogen for plant nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-expressed as a putative FeS maturation factor for AnfH in plant mitochondria, but active holo-AnfH was still not obtained directly from plants.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NifS was co-expressed with AnfH and NifU in plant mitochondria. 2. Its intended role was to support [Fe4S4] cluster maturation for AnfH. 3. Co-expression did not yield active as-isolated NbAnfH, so its sufficiency in planta was not supported.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed transiently in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression in plant transient system; indirect functional inference from AnfH maturation outcome
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential accessory factor for future nitrogenase engineering stacks, but additional components or strategies are likely needed.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Insufficient by itself with NifU to achieve active AnfH in plant mitochondria.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as supportive maturation factor, not direct core metabolic catalyst for ammonia formation in the assays.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioavailable reduced nitrogen for plant nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Computationally designed AnfH variant V6 greatly improves soluble accumulation in plant mitochondria and, after [Fe4S4] reconstitution, donates electrons to AnfDKG to support 15N2 reduction to ammonia; V7 is partially functional and wild type performs poorly.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wild-type A. vinelandii AnfH is largely insoluble and low-yield when expressed in N. benthamiana mitochondria. 2. Designed variants V1-V8 all improved solubility relative to wild type, with V6 (T200A/T228V/E234H) giving ~90-fold higher soluble abundance than wild type. 3. Purified NbAnfH V6 was inactive as isolated but became active after [Fe4S4] cluster reconstitution with EcNifU, supporting acetylene reduction with AvNifDK and 15N2 reduction with AvAnfDKG to near AvAnfH control levels.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: nitrogenase Fe protein family; [Fe4S4]-binding ATP-dependent reductase; InterPro IPR005977
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaf mitochondria
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: electrons from reductant/ATP-dependent electron transfer to AnfDKG; N2 is reduced by partner catalytic component rather than directly by AnfH
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: reduced AvAnfDKG enabling ammonia formation from N2
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ATP-dependent electron donor protein that transfers electrons via its [Fe4S4] cluster to the Fe-only nitrogenase catalytic component AnfDKG, enabling reduction of N2 to ammonia
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required cofactor-containing electron donor required for target product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; solubility western blot; purification; in vitro [Fe4S4] reconstitution with EcNifU; acetylene reduction assay with AvNifDK; 15N2 reduction assay with AvAnfDKG
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key required component for Fe-only nitrogenase activity in planta expression context; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plant mitochondrial targeting; transient leaf expression; anaerobic in vitro reconstitution and activity assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AnfH V6 is a strong engineering module for plant nitrogenase reconstruction because it retains function while markedly improving soluble abundance in plant mitochondria; useful for stacking into synthetic nitrogen fixation pathways, though FeS cluster loading remains a major bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As isolated from plant mitochondria the protein is in apo form and inactive; FeS cluster loading/maturation is inadequate in planta; some substitutions may affect interaction or maturation; wild type and some variants show lower activity or poor recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core experimentally optimized entity is the engineered variant AnfH V6 carrying T200A, T228V, and E234H. V7 (T200A/T228V/G241R) is more soluble than wild type but much less active after reconstitution. V8 with only T228V improves abundance but less than V6. No natural protein identical to V6 was found by BLAST.
